preamble: The shift from the high-front vowel [i] to a glottal stop [ʔ] is a form of glottal reinforcement. It is frequently observed at word boundaries where a 'hard attack' is used to separate the vowel from the preceding context.

examples.note: Glottal stop insertion to resolve hiatus between two vowels.
Consistent glottal stop reinforcement for word-initial vowels.
